TvEE RL seeks 'TEE reviewers for attested model-evaluation boundaries' and Trustless TEE offers exactly trust-boundary review for TEE-based projects; both sit on the trusted-execution/attested-workflow side and share the question of where unverifiable trust enters an attested RL run.

Daedalus offers an open backtesting/evaluation pipeline (orderbook ingestion, correlation backtests) and needs verifiable evaluation it can trust; TvEE RL's attested training/evaluation workflows address exactly the auditability gap — making a Daedalus backtest inspectable rather than an unverifiable offline claim.

Both treat TEE remote attestation (via dstack/Phala) as an admission primitive that displaces reputation — Bitrouter for permissionless LLM-provider onboarding, TeeSQL for attestation-gated mesh membership — so they can share a quote-verification/attestation-registry scheme rather than build two.

TvEE RL lists conclave as a dependency and needs verifiable attested agent/RL workflow plumbing; Conclave's open-source modular skill framework is exactly the reusable runtime TvEE would otherwise re-implement for its attested workflows.

Daedalus declares crossroads a dependency and needs cross-chain asset-movement rails for its delta-neutral hedge thesis; Crossroads offers documented cross-chain asset-movement patterns and signing-committee approaches for teams prototyping such flows.

DCNet lists crossroads as a dependency and offers private-broadcast primitives 'without attribution leakage' — relevant to Crossroads' signing committee, where coordinating signers across chains can leak who initiated a transaction even when payloads are private.

Declared dependency; Shake's shake-to-connect creates a mutual link that needs a lightweight identity handshake, and Crossroads brings identity and policy-constrained signing expertise (its skill_areas include identity) that could harden how a Shake connection is represented and consented to.
